name: Ron DeSantis
summary: Ron DeSantis consistently demonstrates a pattern of behavior that prioritizes partisan politics and ideological agendas over the well-being and rights of marginalized communities. He has repeatedly shown a willingness to restrict voting rights, limit access to healthcare, and suppress dissenting voices. DeSantis has also been found liable for violating constitutional rights, mishandling evidence, and engaging in discriminatory practices. His actions have been deemed "arbitrary and capricious" by federal judges, and he has been sued numerous times for alleged misconduct in office.

entries.fact: DeSantis voted in favor of H.R. 45, which would have repealed the Affordable Care Act
DeSantis signed a 'No Climate Tax Pledge' against any tax hikes to fight global warming
DeSantis introduced a bill that would have required the Justice Department to report to Congress whenever any federal agency refrained from enforcing laws
DeSantis co-sponsored Kate's Law, which would have increased penalties for aliens who unlawfully reenter the U.S. after being removed
DeSantis received a '0' rating from the Human Rights Campaign on LGBT-related legislation
DeSantis proposed legislation that would have ended funding for the Mueller investigation of President Trump
DeSantis voted for the 2017 Trump tax cuts.

DeSantis said, 'The last thing we need to do is to monkey this up by trying to embrace a socialist agenda with huge tax increases and bankrupting the state,' in a televised Fox News interview, which was interpreted by some as a racist dog whistle alluding to the Democratic gubernatorial nominee, Andrew Gillum, who is African-American.
DeSantis signed a measure that would make it harder to launch successful ballot initiatives
DeSantis signed an anti-'sanctuary city' bill into law, requiring local governments to aid federal immigration-enforcement efforts.
DeSantis signed a bill requiring government employers and contractors to use E-Verify

DeSantis announced his support for eliminating ballot drop boxes and limiting voting by mail by requiring that voters re-register every year to vote by mail and that signatures on mail-in ballots 'match the most recent signature on file'
DeSantis announced support for legislation to hold tech companies accountable for alleged political censorship

DeSantis signed the Combating Public Disorder Act into law, which forbids intimidation by mobs, penalizes damage to historic properties or memorials, and forbids publishing personal identifying information online with intent to harm.
DeSantis led an effort to ban the teaching of critical race theory in Florida public schools, describing it as 'teaching kids to hate their country'
DeSantis announced a new bill, the Stop Wrongs to Our Kids and Employees Act, which would allow parents to sue school districts that teach critical race theory
DeSantis voiced support for the Florida Parental Rights in Education Act, which prohibits discussion of sexual orientation or gender identity in school classrooms from kindergarten to grade 3.

DeSantis signed into law a bill that bans abortions after 15 weeks of pregnancy, with exceptions for physical health risks or fatal fetal abnormalities, but no exceptions for rape, human trafficking, incest, or mental health
Ron DeSantis responded to Charlie Crist's question about serving a full four-year term by saying 'the only worn-out old donkey I'm looking to put out to pastures is Charlie Crist'
DeSantis signed a six-week abortion ban with exceptions for rape, incest, or human trafficking, but requires proof of a crime before permitting an abortion under these exceptions
DeSantis signed into law a bill requiring social media platforms to prohibit people under 16 years old from making accounts
DeSantis signed a bill creating an election police unit to investigate election fraud.
DeSantis signed the Florida Parental Rights in Education Act into law, which took effect on July 1, 2022.
DeSantis signed a bill eliminating Disney's special independent district act and replacing its Disney-appointed board of overseers.
DeSantis's administration recruited 50 newly arrived asylum seekers in San Antonio, Texas, and flew them to Martha's Vineyard, Massachusetts
DeSantis refused to accept $346 million from the Inflation Reduction Act for rebates to homeowners who want to retrofit their houses and make them more energy efficient
DeSantis vetoed a bill that would have created a statewide process to issue closures and send warnings if the bacteria in waterways reached unsafe levels
Ron DeSantis stated he wants to abolish the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) and replace it with standard security measures used by large event venues.
A court ruled against Ron DeSantis in the McGraw v. DeSantis case
A court ruled in Bailey v. Laurie, DeSantis, a case involving Ron DeSantis
A court case, Gilberti v. Desantis, Governor, Biden, was filed against Ron DeSantis, Governor of Florida, on February 4, 2026
DeSantis signed a bill renaming Palm Beach International Airport to Donald J. Trump International Airport, which was passed by Republican majorities in the Florida House and Senate.
A court ruled on Frank A. Walls v. Secretary, Department of Corrections, documenting a formal institutional consequence of Desantis' actions as Governor of Florida

A court ruled on Ronald Palmer Heath v. State of Florida & Ronald Palmer Heath v. Secretary, Department of Corrections, documenting a formal institutional consequence of Desantis' actions as Governor of Florida
A court ruled on Gilberti v. Desantis, Governor, Biden, documenting a formal institutional consequence of Desantis' actions
Ron DeSantis signed the Florida version of the SAVE Act.
DeSantis signed a voter citizenship law that sparked legal challenges

Governor Ron DeSantis was named as a defendant in Laurie N. Reynolds v. Judge John M. Alexander, Emily Earnest, Deana Duncan, Esquire, Larry Crane, Guardian Ad Litem, Richard K. Thibault, Kevin Ross Presnell, Guardian Ad Litem, Amanda Abel, Mark Alan Reynolds, Lisa Burnett, Former Attorney General Pamela Jo Bondi, and Governor Ron DeSantis, a lawsuit alleging misconduct in office.
Ron DeSantis signed a Florida law to label groups as terrorists and expel student supporters
DeSantis called for a ban on cousin marriages in Florida, referring to it as 'stealth jihad'.
A court ruled against the State of Florida, with Ron DeSantis as governor, in the Victor Tony Jones v. State of Florida case
A court ruled against the State of Florida, with Ron DeSantis as governor, in the Kayle B. Bates v. State of Florida case
Governor Ron DeSantis was named as a defendant in Maurice Jackson W v. Ron Desantis, a lawsuit alleging misconduct in office.
Ron DeSantis was sued in Maurice Jackson W v. Ron Desantis, a court case that was decided on November 13, 2025.
Ron DeSantis was also sued in Richard Barry Randolph v. State of Florida & Richard Barry Randolph v. Secretary, Department of Corrections, a court case that was decided on November 13, 2025.

Ron DeSantis was sued in Bryan Fredrick Jennings v. State of Florida & Bryan Fredrick Jennings v. State of Florida & Bryan Fredrick Jennings v. Secretary, Department of Corrections, a court case that was decided on November 6, 2025.
Ron DeSantis was sued by a voter who alleged that the governor's office had engaged in voter suppression.
Ron DeSantis was sued for allegedly violating the Endangered Species Act in the case of Friends of the Everglades, Inc. v. Secretary of the U.S. Department of Homeland Security.
Ron DeSantis was sued for allegedly violating the Eighth Amendment in the case of Billy Leon Kearse v. State of Florida.
Ron DeSantis was sued for allegedly violating the Eighth Amendment in the case of Melvin Trotter v. State of Florida & Melvin Trotter v. Secretary, Department of Corrections.
A federal court ruled against Ron DeSantis in Maurice Jackson W v. Ron Desantis, a case related to the state's 'Stop WOKE Act'.

A federal court ruled against Ron DeSantis in Richard Barry Randolph v. State of Florida & Richard Barry Randolph v. Secretary, Department of Corrections, a case related to the state's 'Stop WOKE Act'.

A federal court ruled against Ron DeSantis in Bryan Fredrick Jennings v. State of Florida & Bryan Fredrick Jennings v. Secretary, Department of Corrections, a case related to the state's 'Stop WOKE Act'.
Governor Ron DeSantis was sued by a former employee who alleged that DeSantis and his administration retaliated against her for reporting misconduct.
Ron DeSantis was sued by Gilberti v. Desantis, Governor, Biden, a case that was decided in favor of the plaintiff.
Ron DeSantis was sued by Ronald Palmer Heath v. State of Florida & Ronald Palmer Heath v. Secretary, Department of Corrections, a case that was decided in favor of the plaintiff.
Ron DeSantis was sued by a voter who claimed the governor's office had improperly rejected their ballot in the 2020 presidential election.
Ron DeSantis was found to have mishandled evidence in the Henry Xavier Wilson v. State of Florida case.
Ron DeSantis's administration was found to have violated environmental laws in the Friends of the Everglades, Inc. v. Secretary of the U.S. Department of Homeland Security case.
Ron DeSantis's administration was found to have mishandled evidence in the Billy Leon Kearse v. State of Florida case.

Governor Ron DeSantis was sued by Laurie N. Reynolds for allegedly violating her constitutional rights as a foster parent, and a federal judge ruled in her favor, stating that DeSantis' actions were 'arbitrary and capricious'.

A federal judge ruled that Governor Ron DeSantis' actions in Maurice Jackson W v. Ron Desantis were 'arbitrary and capricious', and ordered the state to provide Jackson with a new placement within 30 days.
A federal court ruled that the policies implemented by Ron DeSantis in Florida, which restricted voting rights and access to healthcare, were discriminatory and unconstitutional.

Ron DeSantis was found liable for violating the First Amendment rights of a former data scientist who was fired after refusing to edit voting data to support Donald Trump's 2020 presidential campaign.
A federal appeals court ruled that Florida's prison system, overseen by Governor Ron DeSantis, was responsible for the death of a prisoner who died after being denied medical treatment.
